data.title: Metacon gets 50M SEK temporary project financing from Fenja Capital to cover working capital needs.
data.description: On January 20, 2026, Metacon AB (publ), a company based in Stockholm, announced it has obtained temporary project financing at market rates. The company received a total of 50 million Swedish Krona (SEK) in gross proceeds from Fenja Capital II A/S. This loan is specifically designed to address Metacon's current working capital requirements.

data.title: CRISP & GREEN partners with Olympic Gold Medalist Shawn Johnson East & NFL player Andrew East in Nashville.
data.description: CRISP & GREEN® has recently revealed a collaboration with Olympic Gold Medalist Shawn Johnson East and former NFL player Andrew East.

data.title: Kodiak launches protein-packed Overnight Oats in three flavors, expanding its product line.  #BreakfastInnovation
data.description: Today in Park City, Utah, Kodiak, a leading brand in high-protein, whole-grain breakfast and snack products, has revealed its newest venture into the market with three protein-rich flavors in a brand-new category of Overnight Oats. This innovation aims to provide consumers with a convenient and nutritious option for their morning routines. The announcement was made on January 20, 2026, showing Kodiak's commitment to offering wholesome and satisfying products.

data.title: Approximately 196,600 Jewish Holocaust survivors alive globally; 50% in Israel, 18% in North America, 17% in Western Europe.
data.description: Recent data has found Jewish Holocaust survivors in over 90 countries worldwide. A significant 50 percent of survivors are living in Israel, while 18 percent are located in North America and 17 percent in Western Europe. This information was released on Jan. 20, 2026, setting the stage for International Holocaust Remembrance Day and the 81st commemoration of this tragic event.

data.title: Regions Financial Corp (NYSE: RF) Q4 earnings fall short with $0.57 EPS and $1.921B revenue, missing estimates. CEO expects growth in strong markets.
data.description: Regions Financial Corp (NYSE: RF) disclosed disappointing fourth-quarter results on Friday. The company's quarterly earnings of 57 cents per share fell short of the expected 61 cents per share, while quarterly sales amounted to $1.921 billion, missing the anticipated $1.936 billion. John Turner, Chairman, President, and CEO of Regions Financial Corp, highlighted the solid growth achieved in 2025, despite the competitive landscape and firm's presence in strong markets. For more information, please visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: RFU researcher receives $1.9M NIH grant for neurological disease study in North Chicago.
data.description: On January 20, 2026, North Chicago-based Rosalind Franklin University (RFU) revealed that Hongkyun Kim, PhD, a professor of cell biology and anatomy at RFU's Center for Cancer Cell Biology, Immunology, and Infection, secured a notable achievement. Kim obtained a prestigious 5-year grant from the National Institutes of Health (NIH). The grant serves as a demonstration of recognition and support for Kim's research endeavors in these critical areas.

data.title: Portage Community Bank mourns passing of Chief Lending Officer, Eric M. Decker. Jan. 20, 2026, Ohio.
data.description: Ravenna, Ohio - On January 20, 2026, Portage Community Bank (PCB), a bank subsidiary of Portage Bancshares, Inc. (PBI), shared the unfortunate news of Eric M. Decker's passing. Decker served as the Senior Vice President and Chief Lending Officer (CLO) at PCB. The organization expressed deep sorrow at the loss of Mr. Decker.

ARMSTRONG STILL OPTIMISTIC ON U.S. CRYPTO BILL Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong says Congress can still pass a comprehensive crypto law, even after he withdrew support for last week’s draft. He opposed the version stalled in the Senate over concerns it favored traditional finance

data.title: AltaSea adds Andrew Zimmern and Ashlan Cousteau to Board of Trustees in LA, influential figures to join the board in 2026.
data.description: In Los Angeles, AltaSea at the Port of Los Angeles, known as the world's top tech hub for the Blue Economy, reported the addition of acclaimed chef Andrew Zimmern and environmental journalist Ashlan Cousteau to its Board of Trustees. Zimmern, an Emmy and Four-Time James Beard Award winner, along with Cousteau, will commence their board roles in the initial meeting of 2026. The esteemed duo will bring their expertise in advocacy and education to further the organization's mission.

data.title: Trump Media & Technology Group Corp. (NASDAQ: DJT) stock is up as they outlined plans for digital token initiative with 2026 cut-off date.
data.description: The stock of Trump Media & Technology Group Corp. (NASDAQ: DJT) saw an increase in trading on Tuesday as the company revealed details about its digital token project. The company, which operates Truth Social and Truth+, has set February 2, 2026, as the deadline for its newly introduced digital tokens. Shareholders are required to have a minimum of one token by that date to participate. Trump Media & Technology stock has been experiencing significant growth, indicating strong investor interest in the initiative. For more information, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Protext Mobility, Inc. (OTC: TXTM) plans to uplist with Deloitte's help, enhancing its readiness for growth.
data.description: Protext Mobility, Inc. (stock symbol: TXTM) is excited to announce a significant development, making progress towards meeting requirements for potential listing on a higher stock exchange, through an initial engagement with Deloitte.

data.title: Sakrete commemorates 90 years with Amerimix at World of Concrete 2026, showcasing legacy in construction industry.
data.description: In Las Vegas on January 20, 2026, Sakrete, the original bagged concrete mix brand, along with sister brand Amerimix, showcased its significant history in aiding residential and industrial building and construction. As a leader in the industry, Sakrete is commemorating its 90th anniversary, affirming its longstanding commitment to providing high-quality products for various construction needs.

data.title: Cordatus Capital acquires ETAS through Red Dot Buildings in Mineola, TX. ETAS makes metal building components.
data.description: In a recent development on January 20, 2026, Cordatus Capital ("Cordatus") and Red Dot Buildings ("Red Dot") have successfully completed the acquisition of East Texas Architectural Sheetmetal ("ETAS"). ETAS, headquartered in Mineola, TX, specializes in the production of metal building components such as purlins and roofing materials. This acquisition marks a significant move within the industry and demonstrates the ongoing consolidation trend. The financial figures and other specific details of the acquisition were not disclosed in the announcement.
